tldr: The team discussed Q4 roadmap priorities and budget allocation. Key decisions were made about mobile-first strategy and hiring timeline.
executive_summary: In this product planning meeting, the team reviewed Q4 priorities focusing on mobile app development. Sarah presented data showing 68% of user traffic now comes from mobile devices, making a compelling case for mobile-first strategy. John initially had concerns about timeline compression but Maria proposed a phased approach. Budget discussions resulted in a $250K allocation, with $150K earmarked for two new frontend developer hires and $100K for infrastructure. Lisa raised critical enterprise churn concerns — 47 accounts threatening to leave due to API reliability issues, representing $2.1M ARR at risk.
detailed_summary: The product planning meeting covered Q4 roadmap prioritization with all key stakeholders present — Sarah (VP Product), John (Engineering Lead), Maria (Design Lead), Tom (Marketing Director), and Lisa (Customer Success). Sarah presented data showing 68% of user traffic now comes from mobile devices, making a compelling case for mobile-first strategy. John initially had concerns about timeline compression but Maria proposed a phased approach: MVP by November 15th, followed by iterative improvements. Budget discussions resulted in a $250K allocation, with $150K earmarked for two new frontend developer hires and $100K for infrastructure and tooling. Tom outlined a full marketing campaign including influencer partnerships and a Product Hunt launch. Lisa raised a critical concern: 47 enterprise customers threatening to churn due to 99.2% uptime falling short of their 99.9% expectations, representing $2.1M in annual recurring revenue at risk. The team agreed to a targeted reliability sprint to address the most critical failure points.

action_items.task: Draft mobile app MVP specification document
Post job listings for 2 frontend developers
Communicate reliability improvements to enterprise clients
Conduct user research for mobile features

decisions.decision: Adopt mobile-first development strategy for Q4
decisions.context: 68% of user traffic comes from mobile devices
Bridge solution while full-time hiring cycle completes

decisions.decision: Cut group chat from social MVP — ship profiles, DMs, activity feeds only
decisions.context: Reduces scope to meet February 28 deadline

decisions.decision: Reliability sprint starts January 15th targeting 99.7% uptime
decisions.context: Protects $2.1M ARR from 47 at-risk enterprise accounts

decisions.decision: Marketing launch date: February 28 with February 14 feature freeze
decisions.context: Aligns with holiday shopping season and prepared campaign

health_explanations.clarity: Communication was mostly clear across 5 speakers, though timeline specifics had 2 ambiguous directives
health_explanations.decisions_made: 6 concrete decisions were reached, each with clear ownership and rationale documented
health_explanations.participation: Sarah dominated ~40% of speaking time; Lisa and Maria had limited contributions compared to others
health_explanations.actionability: 10 clear action items identified with owners assigned, though 3 lack specific calendar deadlines

conflicts.description: John expressed strong concerns about the aggressive Q4 timeline, stating it was 'unrealistic' and requesting it be 'on record'. He pushed back on Sarah's proposed delivery date, citing burnout risk and technical debt from previous rushed timelines. A compromise was eventually reached through contractor hiring.

meeting_archetype.label: Decision Meeting
meeting_archetype.description: 6 major strategic decisions were made with significant budget commitments

follow_up_email:
Subject: Q4 Product Planning — Summary & Action Items

Hi Team,

Thank you for a productive (and spirited!) planning session today. Here's the full recap:

━━━ DECISIONS MADE ━━━
• Mobile-first strategy adopted for Q4 (68% mobile traffic)
• $250K budget allocated — $150K hiring, $100K infrastructure
• 2 senior React contractors to be hired immediately
• Social MVP: profiles + DMs + activity feeds (group chat deferred)
• Reliability sprint starts Jan 15 → target 99.7% uptime
• Marketing launch Feb 28, feature freeze Feb 14

━━━ ACTION ITEMS ━━━
• Sarah → MVP spec by Oct 15
• John → Post frontend dev listings by Oct 10
• John → Contractor requirements by EOD tomorrow
• John → Set up eng-CS Slack channel today
• John → Weekly capacity report starting Friday
• Maria → UI mockups by Oct 20
• Maria → User research by Oct 18
• Tom → Marketing plan by Nov 1
• Lisa → Communicate reliability plan to enterprise clients this week

━━━ NOTE ━━━
John has flagged team capacity concerns — please review the weekly capacity reports carefully. The contractor bridge is critical to maintaining quality.

Weekly check-ins start Monday at 10 AM.

Best regards,
